title: The Kevin Costner Hall of Fame
format: Ahead of 'Horizon,' Sean and Amanda walk Kevin Costner's whole filmography to build a Hall of Fame. They induct ten films, all from a 1987-1996 run, and argue about the rest, including a heated last stand for 'Molly's Game.'

segments.heading: The Kevin Costner Hall of Fame
segments.note: the greens: ten films, every one from 1987-1996

films.title: The Untouchables
films.note: Brian De Palma sees 'Silverado' and casts him as Eliot Ness. First time carrying a movie on his own shoulders, opposite Sean Connery and De Niro. Sean's automatic green; Amanda needed convincing.

films.title: No Way Out
films.note: A remake of 'The Big Clock,' and Amanda's personal must-green: real chemistry with Sean Young, plus Gene Hackman and a wild Will Patton. The naughty-thriller mode Sean wishes he'd done more of.

films.title: Bull Durham
films.note: 'The greenest of the green.' Ron Shelton's jock-poetry works only because Costner sells it. The soulful sexy-jock myth is born here, and he mostly never does it again.

films.title: Field of Dreams
films.note: His 'It's a Wonderful Life': a magical-realist boomer plea, sincere true-believer with a dad wound. Costner himself called it right when the Vanity Fair profiler called it dead on arrival.

films.title: Dances with Wolves
films.note: The $425M bet-on-himself that won Best Picture and Director over 'Goodfellas.' Both hosts find it dull and self-congratulatory, and agree it has to go in anyway.

films.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves
films.note: Miscast, bad accent, start of the troubling hair. Sean couldn't finish a rewatch. In purely because it was a $390M phenomenon that a generation loved (and the Bryan Adams song).

films.title: JFK
films.note: Loquacious, paranoid, obsessed, the furthest thing from his usual register. Sean's pick for his best performance outside 'Bull Durham'; Oliver Stone turns Costner's all-American image against him.

films.title: The Bodyguard
films.note: Not good, and no real chemistry between the leads, but a smash anyway: best-selling soundtrack of all time. Kasdan wrote it for Steve McQueen and Diana Ross. The one they nearly swap out at the end.

films.title: A Perfect World
films.note: Near Sean's top five. Clint Eastwood directs; Costner is a fugitive who bonds with the kid he's abducted. 'If he made a million movies like this he would be our greatest actor.'

films.title: Tin Cup
films.note: The most watchable movie on the list, a warm bath. Reunion with Ron Shelton, credible as a golfer, one of the best golf movies ever after 'Caddyshack.' The latest film in the Hall.

films.title: The Big Chill
films.note: He's not in it. Cast as the friend whose funeral it is, then cut; only his wrist survives, carrying the casket. Kasdan made good with 'Silverado.' The animating myth of his career, so it gets a nostalgic yellow it can't really claim.

films.title: Fandango
films.note: First team-up with writer-director Kevin Reynolds, Amblin-produced. A charming-rogue quartet movie; young-McConaughey energy. He's the best thing in it.

films.title: Silverado
films.note: Kasdan's ode to Ford and Mann. Sean finds it a photocopy, but Costner as the hot, dumb, charismatic one steals it, and it leads to everything.

films.title: Waterworld
films.note: The 'Kevin's Gate' fiasco, 'Mad Max' on water. He pushed the director off and took it over. Yellowed for how much power he had.

films.title: Open Range
films.note: Sean's pick for his best film as a director, beloved by a certain class of person; Robert Duvall is great. Amanda keeps it a yellow to move things along.

films.title: Molly's Game
films.note: Two scenes as Molly's dad, and he walks away with them, dadding out on a park bench. Amanda's heated last stand for a green; Sean grants a grudging yellow.

segments.heading: Walked and cut
segments.note: the rest of the filmography, from the early bit parts to 'Horizon'

films.title: Sizzle Beach, U.S.A.
films.note: One of the early bit parts, an exploitation credit as John Logan.

films.title: Chasing Dreams
films.note: Neither host tracked it down.

films.title: Night Shift
films.note: Ron Howard comedy; Costner is a frat boy partying at the morgue-brothel with no lines.

films.title: Stacy's Knights
films.note: AKA 'The Touch.' Low-budget gambling movie, but directed by future producing partner Jim Wilson and written by 'Dances with Wolves' author Michael Blake, his eventual script fixer.

films.title: Table for Five
films.note: A newlywed husband, no lines.

films.title: Testament
films.note: Small part as a young father in maybe the saddest movie ever made; a nuclear-war drama seen through one town.

films.title: Shadows Run Black
films.note: A slasher Sean couldn't find. Red.

films.title: American Flyers
films.note: John Badham cycling drama; his first sports movie, on the edge of sport and soap.

films.title: Revenge
films.note: Jesse Kornbluth's favorite Costner movie. Nasty, gorgeous Tony Scott crime picture; 'the script is terrible but the story is so good.'

films.title: The Gunrunner
films.note: Neither host has seen it; not on Blu-ray.

films.title: Wyatt Earp
films.note: A three-and-a-half-hour Kasdan Western that 'Tombstone' ate a year earlier. Audiences rejected it.

films.title: The War
films.note: Solid, with a good young Elijah Wood; Costner as a Vietnam vet with PTSD.

films.title: The Postman
films.note: Another three-hour post-apocalyptic bet on the idea of America; a fake mailman doing Shakespeare. A noble failure.

films.title: Message in a Bottle
films.note: Nicholas Sparks weepie with Robin Wright and Paul Newman; a $118M hit that doesn't need to go in.

films.title: For Love of the Game
films.note: Sam Raimi; a credible aging pitcher, but the baseball and the romance don't hold up.

films.title: Play It to the Bone
films.note: A tiny cameo.

films.title: Thirteen Days
films.note: A compelling Cuban Missile Crisis docudrama, but his Boston-Kennedy accent doesn't work.

films.title: 3000 Miles to Graceland
films.note: Elvis-impersonator heist with Kurt Russell; possibly the worst movie he's ever made. Opens on a CGI scorpion, closes on Uncle Kracker.

films.title: Dragonfly
films.note: Tom Shadyac movie neither host really remembers; scrubbed from society.

films.title: The Upside of Anger
films.note: Mike Binder movie built around Joan Allen; Costner as an ex-ballplayer radio host and drinking buddy. One of the great twists.

films.title: Rumor Has It
films.note: Rob Reiner's 'sequel' to 'The Graduate' with Jennifer Aniston. Soderbergh fired the writer ten days in. Amanda's white whale, 'the reddest red,' which she still begs everyone to study.

films.title: The Guardian
films.note: Coast Guard rescue-swimmer movie; the diving sequences rip, everything Ashton Kutcher sinks it.

films.title: Mr. Brooks
films.note: Costner as a serial killer; unavailable to stream, which Sean jokes is on purpose.

films.title: Swing Vote
films.note: A drunk becomes the literal deciding vote in a presidential election. Sean found it reprehensible. Red.

films.title: The New Daughter
films.note: A horror movie so obscure four critic friends had never heard of it. Red by default.

films.title: The Company Men
films.note: 'What about executives?' A very small brother-in-law part; he's the drywall of the movie.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: Jonathan Kent, good casting in a movie Sean despises.

films.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit
films.note: Branagh spy movie; Costner is the stoic CIA recruiter. A watchable two-and-a-half-star film.

films.title: 3 Days to Kill
films.note: McG / Luc Besson assassin-with-cancer shoot-em-up. Not good.

films.title: Draft Day
films.note: Entertainingly bad; not how any of this works. Features friends Griffin Newman and Tim Simons.

films.title: Black or White
films.note: Binder again; a white grandfather raising his Black grandkids. Wouldn't get made today. Red.

films.title: McFarland, USA
films.note: Earnest Disney cross-country coach movie; fine, bland, red.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: Jonathan Kent again.

films.title: Criminal
films.note: Another crime-movie fiasco.

films.title: Hidden Figures
films.note: The paternal NASA figure; not bad, not in.

films.title: The Highwaymen
films.note: John Lee Hancock's Bonnie-and-Clyde manhunt. Not in.

films.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
films.note: He voices Enzo, the dog.

films.title: Let Him Go
films.note: A pandemic watch; he's actually trying, with a good Lesley Manville. Solid.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: Jonathan Kent, joked in as an 'auto green.'

films.title: Horizon: An American Saga - Chapter 1
films.note: The $38M self-financed Western that occasioned the whole episode; a Friday review with Chris Ryan to come.

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice

The Art of Racing in the Rain.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
